title: ulimit discrepancies
body:
### Issue Description

There seems to be ulimit discrepancies both for the `--ulimit host` setting as well as between` podman run` and `podman create`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. testuser@ubuntu2504:~$ ulimit -Sn; ulimit -Hn
1024
1073741816

2. testuser@ubuntu2504:~$ podman run --rm alpine ash -c 'ulimit -Sn; ulimit -Hn'
1048576
1048576

3. testuser@ubuntu2504:~$ podman run --rm --ulimit host alpine ash -c 'ulimit -Sn; ulimit -Hn'
1048576
1048576

4. testuser@ubuntu2504:~$ podman create --rm --name test alpine sleep 1 > /dev/null && podman start test > /dev/null && podman exec test ash -c 'ulimit -Sn; ulimit -Hn'
1073741816
1073741816

5. testuser@ubuntu2504:~$ podman create --rm --ulimit host --name test alpine sleep 1 > /dev/null && podman start test > /dev/null && podman exec test ash -c 'ulimit -Sn; ulimit -Hn'
1073741816
1073741816

### Describe the results you received

I received the podman default ulimit (`sysconf(_SC_OPEN_MAX)`/`nofile`/`ulimit -n`) when using `--ulimit host`
I received the host hard limit on hard/soft for `podman create`

### Describe the results you expected

I expected to see the host ulimit to be taken from the host when using `--ulimit host`
I expected podman run and podman create to have consistent behaviour. I.e. to not use the host hard limit when using create.

This is from the docs of both run and create:
> If nofile and nproc are unset, a default value of 1048576 will be used, unless overridden in containers.conf(5). However, if the default value exceeds the hard limit for the current rootless user, the current hard limit will be applied instead.
> 
> Use host to copy the current configuration from the host.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Fresh ubuntu 25.04 install.

### Additional information

This may cause quite significant memory problems for any software using e.g. `sysconf(_SC_OPEN_MAX)` in order to preallocate memory when using a distrobox. This since distrobox uses create which sets the very high host hard limit.

comments.body:
This is also somewhat interesting, the behaviour differs from 5.0.3:
```
testuser@ubuntu2410:~$ ulimit -Sn; ulimit -Hn
1024
1073741816
testuser@ubuntu2410:~$ podman create --rm --ulimit host --name test alpine sleep 1 > /dev/null && podman start test > /dev/null && podman exec test ash -c 'ulimit -Sn; ulimit -Hn'
1048576
1048576
testuser@ubuntu2410:~$ podman --version
podman version 5.0.3
```

Thank you, not sure you you want this comment in this bug or #24243 but it may be worth mentioning that setting e.g.:
```
[containers]
default_ulimits = ["nofile=1024:1048576"]
```
in containers.conf alleviates the issue, even when using `--ulimit host` which seems a bit odd (i would have expected host settings to override default).

On another note, this problem will cause erlang/elixir nodes to mmap 4GB of memory (even during compilation) causing both significant slowdown at startup and rendering a `podman create` unusable for a default podman install in certain situations and somewhat wasteful even with `podman run` since the soft limit is still unusually high.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
